| ## Endpoint Protection and Cybersecurity Requirements: /The proposed solution should include a centrally managed enterprise-grade Endpoint Protection Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) platform covering all servers and client workstations within the project scope. The solution shall provide at least: » Real-time threat prevention and detection - » Malware and ransomware protection - » Behavioral analysis and advanced threat analytics - » Automated signature, engine, and policy updates - » Centralized monitoring, reporting, and incident management - » Threat response and containment capabilities » Support and not limited to Microsoft Windows Workstations and Windows - Server operating systems The proposed cybersecurity platform shall be based on CrowdStrike or equivalent enterpriseclass EDR/XDR solutions approved by EAC. Airport Infrastructure Architecture ## Overview | The proposed infrastructure shall support mission-critical airport operational systems across eight airports, providing high availability (HA), resiliency, performance, and future scalability through a standardized architecture that ensures operational consistency, simplified management, and unified deployment across all sites. ## Datacenter Architecture Each airport shall include two physically independent Core Rooms (Core 1 & Core 2) operating in a high-availability architecture interconnected via fiber backbone, ensuring service continuity, resiliency and elimination of any single point of failure (SPOF). ## Redundant Infrastructure Platform **==> picture [484 x 135] intentionally omitted <==** **----- Start of picture text -----**

| ## Datacenter Networking Each datacenter shall deploy two datacenter-grade ToR switches providing fully redundant high-speed connectivity, resilient switching paths and secure segmentation for management, storage and virtualized networks. ## Local Architecture, Backup and Data Protection Each airport shall deploy a redundant local infrastructure with centralized backup and disaster recovery capabilities to ensure business continuity and high availability through: - Network Redundancy: Redundant network devices and communication paths. - Hardware Redundancy: Dual power, UPS, redundant NICs, and RAID storage. - Backup & Storage Redundancy: Centralized backup, replication, retention, and rapid recovery capabilities. The backup platform shall provide reliable data protection and fast recovery against system failure. ## Standardized Multi-Airport Deployment A unified architecture shall be deployed across all eight airports to ensure standardized operations, simplified support and maintenance, consistent resiliency, and scalable future expansion across all Sites. ## Centralized Monitoring Platform A centralized monitoring and control platform located in EAC/COC/CCC will provide aggregated visibility across all airports. This platform will collect operational data, flight information, and system metrics from each airport infrastructure while allowing each airport to maintain operational independence. **==> picture [110 x 66] intentionally omitted <==** **----- Start of picture text -----**

- NN CUPPS System - Scope of work - - The standardization of check-in and boarding counters is a key element of airport management. This standardization was the subject of the IATA recommendation #1 797, which describes the CUPPS concept. - - The Common Use Systems proposed shall strictly comply with this [ATA recommendation (RP) 1797 (1.03 CUPPS release) and ensure a CUTE/CUPPS backward compatibility as a minimum requirement. - - In order to ensure the backward compatibility among all applications and hardware, the Tenderer/Applicant shall bring the evidence that its system can support CUTE system (software, hardware and protocols, including legacy) on the same architecture than the one proposed. - The CUPPS platform provider must demonstrate its ability to keep up with the latest IATA recommendations thus ensuring interfaces harmonization between resources sharing platforms and airlines applications. - - The CUPPS must be certified against the latest CUPPS standard at the time of installation. - The system must comply with the following international standards: - RP 1797b and 1745 related to baggage sorting/reconciliation systems. - RP 740 and 769 for TAGs description. - RP 722c/d for boarding passes format - RP 722e for boarding passes paper description - RP 792 for bar coded boarding passes (BCBP compliant) and allow printing of boarding passes with a 2D barcode (PDF 417) - - RP 1797 describing CUPPS system - The system should also evolve following the standards: IATA’s RP1797, A4A’s TP 30.201 and ACI’s RP 500A07. - - The CUPPS Architecture shall be consistent with IATA’s strategic initiatives including: -
